chapter_title.en: Chapter 56 – The supplication for the illnesses and diseases

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن أو موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/431)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن أو موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/431)
text: إِذَا رَأَيْتَ الرَّجُلَ مَرَّ بِهِ الْبَلَاءُ فَقُلِ.
الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ الَّذِي عَافَانِي مِمَّا ابْتَلَاكَ بِهِ وَ فَضَّلَنِي عَلَيْكَ وَ عَلَى كَثِيرٍ مِمَّنْ خَلَقَ وَ لَا تُسْمِعْهُ

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, and a number of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Muhammad Bin Ismail, altogether from Hanan Bin Sadeyr, from his father,
(It has been narrated) from Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘Whenever you see a man who has been afflicted by the affliction, so say,

‘The Praise is for Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Who Granted me good health from that He<sup>azwj</sup> Afflicted you with, and Preferred me over you, and from a lot of the people’ –  but be sure not to make him hear what you said.’.
en.sarwar: 5. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father and A number of our people have narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad Drust ibn abu Mansur Muhammad ibn Isma’Il all from Hanan ibn Sadir from his father from abu Ja’far (a.s.) who has said the following: “Whenever you see a man afflicted with a misfortune, say, ‘All praise belongs to Allah, Who has kept me safe from that with which you suffer, that He has given me preference over you and over many creatures,’ but be sure not to make him hear what you said.’”
